jobs.title: 🇩🇪Customer Support Manager
jobs.jd_full:
🇩🇪 This job ad is written in German. 🇩🇪

Die Parkosecure GmbH ist ein innovatives Unternehmen im Bereich Parkraummanagement in der Schweiz.

Für die Betreuung unserer Kunden in der französischen Schweiz suchen wir eine zuverlässige und selbständig arbeitende Persönlichkeit im 100% Remote-Modell.

Du arbeitest vollständig ortsunabhängig und bist die zentrale Ansprechperson für unsere französischsprachigen Kunden.

Vos missions

  • Bearbeitung von Kundenanfragen per Telefon und E-Mail

  • Professionelle Behandlung von Kundenreaktionen und Einsprachen
  • Unterstützung im administrativen Bereich
  • Mitarbeit bei Projekten (bei vorhandenen Ressourcen)
  • Sicherstellung einer hohen Kundenzufriedenheit
Vos compétences
  • Französisch Muttersprache (zwingend)
  • Sehr gute schriftliche und mündliche Ausdrucksweise
  • Deutsch und oder Englisch von Vorteil
  • Erfahrung im Kundendienst oder Administration
  • Sehr gute EDV Kenntnisse (MS Office zwingend)
  • Selbständige, strukturierte Arbeitsweise
  • Belastbar und lösungsorientiert
Avantages
  • 100% Remote Arbeit weltweit möglich
  • Flexible Arbeitsgestaltung
  • Dynamisches wachsendes Unternehmen
  • Möglichkeit zur Weiterentwicklung
  • Direkter Einfluss auf Prozesse und Kundenzufriedenheit
  • 5 Wochen Ferien
  • Fixer, überdurchschnittlicher Lohn (kein Bonus, keine Provision)

Wir bieten dir eine stabile und verantwortungsvolle Tätigkeit in einem wachsenden Unternehmen, in dem deine Arbeit geschätzt wird.

Du arbeitest selbständig, bist Teil eines engagierten Teams und übernimmst eine wichtige Rolle in der Betreuung unserer französischsprachigen Kunden.

Wenn du zuverlässig bist, gerne Verantwortung übernimmst und Wert auf eine langfristige Zusammenarbeit legst, freuen wir uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

jobs.title: Product Manager Data Feeds
jobs.jd_full:
About The Role:

Our data feed business is one of the fastest-growing segments of YipitData. To accelerate that growth, we're looking for a Product Manager to own and help scale several of our data feed products.

This is not a maintenance role. We are in the midst of a strategic transformation, moving towards a more centralized, commercially accountable product management model. You will be instrumental in shaping how that model works, establishing best practices, and driving both internal and external impact across a portfolio of high-value alternative data feeds.

Our Feeds organization includes product managers, quant researchers, product specialists, feed operations, and product marketers. We collaborate closely with engineering partners (who own technical architecture, infrastructure, and delivery) and our go-to-market teams (Sales, CS, and dedicated Product Specialists who sit between Product and Sales). Together, we build data feed products on top of proprietary and exclusively licensed alternative datasets - including transaction data, email receipt data, B2B spend data, and more - serving fundamental, quantitative, and systematic investors.

What You'll Do

Product Strategy & Roadmap Ownership

  • Own the product roadmap for one or more data feeds, defining, prioritizing, and evangelizing the what and the why behind what we build
  • Set product vision and strategy that balances both internal customer needs (Quant research team requirements, analyst workflows) and external client needs (buy-side investors, quant/systematic funds)
  • Translate business goals into a technical roadmap in close partnership with engineering, ensuring strategy matches technical reality
  • Contribute to long-term feed portfolio strategy, identifying opportunities for new products, enhancements, and market expansion

Commercial Accountability

  • Share ARR and P&L accountability alongside Product Specialists / Product Enablement partners for the feeds you own.
  • Conduct deep customer and market research to understand

jobs.title: Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
• Demonstrated experience managing multi-workstream data or AI programs with phased delivery, PoCs, acceptance criteria, and go-live support
  • Strong stakeholder management skills across both technical and business audiences
  • Comfortable managing risks, dependencies, budgets, and status reporting up to steering committee level
  • Hands-on experience operating inside Scrum and Jira-based delivery models
  • Ability to coordinate across architecture, data engineering, BI, QA, and business user teams

jobs.title: Product Manager - Internal Products
jobs.jd_full:
We are expanding our B2B banking ecosystem and looking for a Product Manager (Internal Products) to build and scale the internal tools that power our SME operations.

This is a highly execution-focused role. You will work closely with Engineering, Risk, and Operations to improve and automate internal systems that directly impact credit decisions, portfolio performance, and customer experience.

We are looking for someone who can start delivering value within the first 4–6 weeks: defining roadmaps, writing specs, and working with engineering and operations.

What You’ll Do

  • Improve and scale internal tools used by SME operations and credit teams
  • Gather requirements from stakeholders and turn them into clear product specs
  • Write detailed PRDs and manage backlog with Engineering
  • Identify inefficiencies in processes and propose automation solutions
  • Support rollout of new features and ensure adoption by internal teams
  • Bring practical best practices from international fintech / banking markets

Requirements

  • 4–7 years of Product Management experience
  • Hands-on experience building or improving internal/back-office systems
  • Experience in fintech or banking
  • Strong execution mindset — comfortable moving fast and delivering
  • Experience working with cross-functional teams (Engineering, Risk, Ops)
  • Exposure to international fintech/banking markets
  • Fluent English

Benefits

  • Remote work from anywhere in the world
  • Performance bonus, 25+ paid vacations and day-offs, personal development plan
  • Purpose: shape the future of fintech in Nigeria with products that reach millions, backed by a competitive salary
  • Growth: thrive in a fast paced work environment with flexibility, collaboration, and access to cutting-edge tools
  • Join a diverse, inclusive team where all voices are valued, and professional growth is a priority.

jobs.title: Junior Technical Product Owner (w/m/d) - Data
jobs.jd_full:
Location: Hamburg, Berlin, Gütersloh oder Remote (idealerweise max. 3 Stunden Reisezeit nach Berlin)

Hey Data-Mind! 👋

Wir sind smartclip – das Tech-Unternehmen hinter Europas führender TV- und Video-Werbeplattform.

Unser Ziel? Die europäische TV-Welt mit smarter Technologie und offenen Systemen zukunftsfähig machen. Wenn Du gerne den Überblick behältst, komplexe Systeme magst und Spaß daran hast, Dinge zu verstehen – dann lies weiter.

Deine Rolle

Kurz gesagt: Du bist das technische Rückgrat unserer Data Services – aber keine Sorge, Du musst nicht alles schon wissen.
Als Junior Technical Product Owner (w/m/d) sorgst Du dafür, dass unsere Microservices nicht nur laufen, sondern rennen. Du bringst Ordnung ins Chaos und hilfst uns, stabile und skalierbare Software zu bauen.

Was das konkret heißt:

  • Du übersetzt "Tech" in "Planbar": Du nimmst komplexe Ideen und Anforderungen vom Business und wandelst sie in verständliche, technische Aufgaben für unsere Engineering-Teams um.

  • Mitdenken & Mitschreiben: Du arbeitest Hand in Hand mit unseren Data Engineers, überprüfst Anforderungen und definierst Testcases. Dabei lernst Du täglich etwas über Software-Architekturen und Datenflüsse.

  • Alles im Blick: Du bist der rote Faden im Projekt. Du begleitest die Entwicklung von der ersten Skizze über den Rollout bis hin zum laufenden Betrieb.

  • Digitales Handwerkszeug: Tools wie Git, Jenkins oder Monitoring-Dashboards sind Deine besten Freunde, um den Status aller Services im Blick zu behalten.

Dein Profil

Wir suchen jemanden mit dem richtigen Mindset – das ist wichtiger für uns als bereits ein Experte zu sein.

  • Fundament: Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik oder vergleichbar.

  • Erste Schritte: Du hast erste Erfahrungen als Technical Product Owner/Manager oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle gesammelt – oder bist einfach sehr tech-affin und willst nun ins Produktmanagement einsteigen.

  • Tech-Savvy: Du hast ein gutes technisches Grundverständnis. Begriffe wie TDD, ETL oder Monitoring-Tools (Grafana, Graylog) sind dir nicht komplett fremd.

  • Sprachrohr: Du fühlst Dich in einer agilen Arbeitsweise wohl. Du musst nicht jeden Tag selbst Code schreiben, aber Du verstehst die Sprache der Developer und kannst Probleme gut benennen.

  • Haltung: Du arbeitest gerne eigenständig suchst pragmatische Lösungen und magst ehrliche, klare Kommunikation.

Nice to have: Hast Du schon mal mit Daten gearbeitet, Microservices gesehen oder Dich im AdTech-Umfeld bewegt? Super! Falls nicht, erklären wir es dir gern.

Warum Dich der Job glücklich machen wird

Bei smartclip gibt’s keine verstaubten Strukturen – sondern Raum zum Ausprobieren.

Wir sind überzeugt davon überzeugt, dass gute Technologie da entsteht, wo Leute mit Leidenschaft und Neugier zusammenarbeiten.

  • Ein Team mit Tech-Mindset und Humor.

  • Spannende Projekte & neueste Technologien:Bei uns arbeitest Du an echten Zukunftsthemen – mit modernen Tech-Stacks wie Spark, Prefect, Trino, Scala, Python, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Google Cloud und mehr.

  • Remote-First innerhalb Deutschlands (aber unsere Offices in Berlin, Hamburg & in der Nähe von Gütersloh sind für Dich offen).

  • Keine Angst vor Neuem: Wir probieren aus, was wirklich funktioniert, und keine Frage, die Du stellst, ist zu viel.

  • Eigenverantwortung statt Bürokratie – wir vertrauen darauf, dass Du Dinge anpackst.

  • Top-Equipment:Du bekommst modernste Hardware und Tools – egal ob Mac, Linux oder Dein persönliches Lieblings-Setup.

  • Weiterentwicklung inklusive:Wir investieren in Dich – mit Trainings über Udacity & Coursera, Hackathons, Konferenzen sowie persönlichem Coaching.

Und sonst so?Deutschlandticket-Zuschuss, JobRad, Urban Sports Club, RTL+ Premium-Abo, Mitarbeiterrabatte und coole Team-Events gehören natürlich auch dazu.

Wenn Du Lust hast, Technologie zu gestalten statt sie nur zu verwalten – dann klick auf „apply now“ und werde Teil unseres Data Engineering Teams.

smartclip is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, nationality, age, gender, gender identity, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or any other diverse characteristics.

Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Manager GTM Strategy & Operations
jobs.jd_full:
Kaseya® is the leading provider of complete IT infrastructure and security management solutions for Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and internal IT organizations worldwide powered by AI. Kaseya’s best-in-breed technologies allow organizations to efficiently manage and secure IT to drive sustained business success. Kaseya has achieved sustained, strong double-digit growth over the past several years and is backed by Insight Venture Partners www.insightpartners.com), a leading global private equity firm investing in high-growth technology and software companies that drive transformative change in the industries they serve.

Founded in 2000, Kaseya currently serves customers in over 20 countries across a wide variety of industries and manages over 15 million endpoints worldwide. To learn more about our company and our award-winning solutions, go to www.Kaseya.com and for more information on Kaseya’s culture.

Kaseya is not your typical company. We are not afraid to tell you exactly who we are and our expectations. The thousands of people that succeed at Kaseya are prepared to go above and beyond for the betterment of our customers.

Manager of Strategy, Planning, & Analytics

Who We Are:

At Kaseya, we’re leading the evolution of software for the IT industry through constant innovation. We pride ourselves on delivering outstanding results for our customers, which in turn has led to extraordinary growth. That’s where you come in.

Kaseya is looking for a Manager of Strategy, Planning, & Analytics to focus on strategic and operational initiatives impacting our customer base and customer-facing teams. The ideal candidate for this role is a strategic thinker with an operational bend; they love B2B tech and want to take a role in optimizing our growing business lines. They know how to utilize data-driven insights to shape company priorities and guide the work of multiple departments, not to mention hundreds of co-workers.

This role will support strategy for customer-facing functions, including New Customer Acquisition, Account Management, Marketing, and Product. The role reports directly to the Director of Strategy, Planning, & Analytics. This individual will help influence and drive the strategic direction of the company.

Roles & Responsibilities:

· Drive strategic and operational initiatives impacting our customer base and customer-facing teams

· Develop and lead impactful, measurable strategies that drive revenue growth and meaningful interactions with customers

· Lead workstreams with cross-functional teams as necessary to ensure we are bringing the best thinking to key initiatives

· Manage special projects across functions and departments

Skills & Experience:

· 5+ years’ experience in strategy, finance, investment banking / PE, or consulting

· Expertise in recurring revenue business models is a must have; familiarity with B2B software is a plus

· Strong analytical and modeling skills with ability to work across multiple sources of data; Excel ninja

· Deep familiarity with finance or financial analytics; able to apply a financial lens to all strategic decision-making

· Strong communication and presentation skills with a proven ability to work collaboratively

· Experience leading a team

· MBA or graduate degree in a quantitative field a plus

Join the Kaseya growth rocket ship and see how we are #ChangingLives !

Additional information

Kaseya provides equal employment opportunity to all employees and applicants without regard to race, religion, age, ancestry, gender, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, veteran status, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager AI Care Experience
jobs.jd_full:
About 9amHealth

9amHealth is redefining cardiometabolic care with a first of its kind, whole body approach to preventing and treating diabetes, obesity, high blood pressure, and hypertension. Founded in 2021 and backed by top tier investors including 7Wire Ventures, Human Capital, Founders Fund, and Define Ventures, we deliver custom care plans, fast medication delivery, and expert guidance that empower patients to live healthier lives.

We believe AI enabled health services will be among the biggest winners of this technology shift, and we’re building accordingly. We already use AI across the product: member facing agents, clinical copilots, automated triage, intelligent care workflows. Our goal is to bring the cost of healthcare down dramatically while providing an incredible experience for both our members and clinicians. AI isn’t a feature on our roadmap, it’s how we think about every feature on our roadmap.

We’re a Series B company with over 200 people, growing fast. Our product and engineering team is distributed across the US and Austria, and we’re scaling it significantly.

The Role

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own and evolve our AI Care Experience—how care is delivered to members through software.

This role requires deep experience in healthcare or care delivery environments, as you will directly shape how clinical workflows, decision-making, and care pathways are translated into product and AI systems.

You will lead a product team and be responsible for the end-to-end member journey, using AI to make care more personalized, proactive, and scalable, while maintaining strong clinical quality, safety, and trust.

You will operate at the intersection of consumer product, AI systems, and clinical care, helping turn healthcare delivery into high-quality, software-driven experiences.

What You’ll Do

Own the End to End Care Experience

  • Drive the evolution of the member journey into a seamless, AI first care experience

  • Identify where AI can reduce friction, improve clarity, and drive behavior change

  • Balance automation with human care to ensure strong clinical outcomes and member trust

Translate Care into Product Systems

  • Partner closely with clinical teams to understand care pathways, decision logic, and real world workflows

  • Translate these into product experiences, AI behavior, and system logic

  • Help define how care is delivered through software, not just supported by it

Build AI Enabled Care Experiences

  • Ship experiences powered by:

    • conversational interfaces and agents

    • personalized recommendations and nudges

    • intelligent workflows and automation

  • Think in systems across the full care journey, not isolated features

Define Human vs AI Boundaries

  • Determine when AI should act vs when to escalate to clinicians

  • Design for clinical safety, appropriateness, and trust in all member interactions

AI Product Craft (Prompting & Evals)

  • Contribute to prompt design and iteration for key care interactions

  • Help define and run evaluation frameworks (evals) to measure AI quality, safety, and effectiveness

  • Partner with engineering and data teams to continuously improve system performance

Outcome Driven Product Development

  • Own metrics across engagement, retention, and clinical adherence

  • Use experimentation and data to improve both UX and AI system behavior

Product Strategy & Execution

  • Own roadmap and prioritization for the AI Care Experience

  • Lead cross-functional execution across product, design, engineering, and clinical teams

  • Balance rapid iteration with long-term product quality, safety, and scalability

What You’ll Need

Minimum Qualifications (Required)

  • 5+ years of Product Management experience in consumer or SaaS products

  • Direct experience in healthcare or care delivery environments (required)

    • Examples: digital health, care delivery organizations, clinical workflows, patient-facing health products

  • Strong track record owning end to end user or patient experiences

  • Experience working on or alongside AI powered products

  • Proven ability to work closely with clinicians and translate care pathways and decision logic into product systems

  • Strong product instincts around UX, behavior change, and engagement in high stakes environments

  • Comfort operating in fast moving, ambiguous environments

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience building AI native or AI first healthcare products

  • Familiarity with prompting, evals, or AI system iteration

  • Experience designing human in the loop systems in clinical settings

  • Startup or high growth environment experience

Compensation for this role is targeted up to a base salary of $180,000, depending on experience and location.

What you can expect at 9amHealth

At 9amHealth, you’ll join the exciting environment of a startup with the stability of highly experienced founders that have successfully built a business from scratch. We foster a collaborative learning mindset and a passion for improvement. You’ll work with a brand-new product alongside a small, flexible team of people eager to shape technology, infrastructure, and culture.

Our team members are spread across the globe, with physical sites in San Diego, California, and Vienna, Austria, but we value your culture regardless of where you work.

We offer a comprehensive benefits package, including health, dental, and vision insurance, along with flexible PTO and work from home options. We provide a professional development budget and support continuing education, empowering our team to grow alongside the company. Team members have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact on chronic disease management and patient outcomes while contributing to a collaborative, mission driven culture focused on improving healthcare accessibility and affordability.

Our commitment to diversity and inclusion

Diabetes disproportionately impacts BIPOC (black, indigenous, and people of color) communities. We are committed to creating a workforce that reflects our patients and creating a positive healthcare experience for them. We welcome applications from people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, people with disabilities, members of ethnic minorities, and foreign born residents.

jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
US Mobile is building the future of wireless communication. The goal: one unified network open to any person and any device, worldwide. Connection without walls.

We’re getting there by empowering customers. All three major networks on one phone and one plan, plus home internet from Starlink. No lock-in. No commitments. Custom fit plans at every price point. 24⁄7 customer support with real people, empowered to help. We get real-time feedback from Reddit, surveys, and customer support informing product roadmaps and everything we do. It’s working — Consumer Reports named us the top-rated mobile carrier two years in a row.*

And we’re building innovative systems that scale. A network agnostic tech stack. Agile, cross-functional teams built on trust and mutual respect. This work isn’t for everyone. If you work fast, flexibly, and collaboratively — without compromising standards — we want to hear from you.

The Opportunity

This is a Staff or Principal level Product Manager role with end-to-end ownership of Devices & and our digital shopping Marketplace, including defining what best-in-class digital shopping looks like for the next generation of connected consumers. You’ll set the bar for how connectivity devices are discovered, evaluated, and purchased online, then back it with the operational infrastructure to deliver: inventory, fulfillment, reverse logistics, and everything in between.

You’ll own adjacent programs too, protection, trade-ins, upgrades, and financing, and be responsible for the full arc from 0-to-1 design through scaled operations. This is a business-critical surface with direct impact on conversion, revenue, and retention and a true growth lever for the Company.

You’ll work closely with the leadership team to set direction, and partner across Growth, Operations, and Engineering to bring it to life. Success in this role requires strong judgment, a bias for action, and the ability to drive clarity and outcomes in complex, cross-functional environments.

We expect PMs to operate with a high degree of ownership. That means making principled decisions with imperfect information, aligning teams around clear direction, and holding a high bar for execution and results.

What You’ll Do:

  • Define and own the multi-year roadmap for Devices & eCommerce Marketplace, setting strategy, aligning stakeholders, and translating vision into shipped products.
  • Lead device-adjacent programs including protection plans, trade-ins, financing, and upgrade cycles — from 0-to-1 design through scaled operations.
  • Partner with Growth and Marketing to design device promotions, launch experiments, and drive conversion improvements across the acquisition funnel.
  • Drive revenue, conversion, and margin through rigorous data analysis, A/B testing, and continuous iteration.
  • Build logistics and fulfillment capabilities — working across engineering and operations to handle inventory, order management, and reverse logistics at scale.
  • Define and evolve the supplier and partner ecosystem, including negotiating with OEMs, distributors, and third-party service providers.
  • Collaborate deeply with Engineering, Design, Customer Support, and Operations to deliver cohesive, high-quality experiences.
  • Operate as a force multiplier for your teams — removing blockers, making hard calls, and keeping execution on track.

What We’re Looking For:

  • 7+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years in a senior or staff-level IC role with meaningful scope and ownership.
  • Demonstrated track record of shipping high-impact products end-to-end
  • Deep experience in digital shopping, marketplace, eCommerce, or leading consumer tech platforms.
  • Strong analytics and data-driven decision making experience
  • Growth and experimentation background; you’ve designed and shipped A/B tests, conversion experiments, or monetization changes.
  • Experience negotiating with hardware vendors, OEMs, or commercial partners.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

How we Work:

PMs at US Mobile are strategic builders, cross-functional leaders and hands-on owners. You’ll be expected to get deep and zoom out just as fast to communicate strategy to leadership. We move quickly, we hold high bars, and we care deeply about our Customers.

We’re a fast-paced, highly collaborative, fun environment packed with highly motivated teammates.

Compensation and Benefits:

  • $175,000 – $215,000 base salary (NYC-based, commensurate with experience)
  • Equity participation
  • Free cellular service — every US Mobile plan, on the best networks in the country
  • Free lunch daily in our NYC office + fully stocked kitchen
  • Monthly gym reimbursement and Omnycard reimbursement
  • Flexible hours within a hybrid schedule (Mon–Wed in-office)

$175,000 - $215,000 a year

Think you could be a good fit? Apply to learn more!

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

jobs.title: Senior Business Operations AI Program Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Senior Business Operations AI Program Manager(Hybrid - 3 days per week onsite)

Overview:

The Senior Business Operations AI Program Manager is a high-impact role responsible for supporting AI forward operational processes across the functional departments at Weedmaps. This role works closely with cross functional teams to understand their operations then plan and implement AI based solutions in partnership with key technology org stakeholders. As a senior strategic partner, you will bridge the gap between executive AI vision and tangible operational reality, ensuring that AI operational deployment is aligned with overarching business goals.

The impact you’ll make:

  • AI Program Management: Lead the end-to-end lifecycle of AI programs, from identifying high-impact operational use cases to full-scale deployment and continuous optimization.
  • AI Enablement & Workflow Integration: Design and implement scalable operational frameworks that incorporate AI into existing business workflows to improve team velocity, resource allocation, and decision-making accuracy.
  • Cross-Functional Orchestration: Act as the primary technical-operational bridge between technology org AI stakeholders and business stakeholders, ensuring AI is effectively tuned to solve departmental bottlenecks.
  • AI Governance & Ethics: Collaborate with Legal and Information Security to ensure all AI deployments adhere to the Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence Technology Policy, focusing on data privacy, bias mitigation, and regulatory compliance.
  • Advanced Data Analytics: Utilize AI-driven insights and business metrics to monitor the health of ongoing programs, providing predictive reporting to leadership on the ROI of AI enablement.
  • Stakeholder Management: Manage expectations and resolve high-level conflicts across diverse executive teams, serving as the subject matter expert on how AI can augment human workforce capabilities.
  • Change Management for the AI Era: Drive organizational culture shifts toward AI-native operations, implementing training programs and feedback loops to ensure seamless human-AI collaboration.
  • Operational Excellence: Continuously evaluate the evolving AI landscape to identify new AI-based technologies that can further streamline internal processes and support market expansion.

What you’ve accomplished:

  • Experience: 5+ years of experience in program management, business operations, or management consulting, preferably within a SaaS or high-growth tech environment with at least 2 years specifically focused on AI or automation initiatives.
  • AI Expertise: Demonstrated experience in deploying AI solutions, specifically large language model (LLM) applications, AI agents, or automated decision-support systems.
  • Technical Proficiency: Advanced skills in project management tools (Jira, Confluence) and a deep understanding of AI-related technical stacks, APIs, and data visualization tools like Tableau.
  • Strategic Problem-Solving: A proven track record of creating structure in highly ambiguous environments and leading complex, high-stakes organizational pivots.
  • Communication: Exceptional ability to distill complex AI and operational concepts into clear, executive-level strategies and summaries.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, or Computer Science;

Bonus points:

  • A solid understanding of the cannabis tech landscape or experience in a similarly highly-regulated industry

The base pay range for this position is $126,765 - $155,000 per year

2026 US Benefits for Full Time, Regular Employees:

  • Physical Health (Medical, Dental & Vision)

    • 100% employer-paid premium for employees
    • Up to 80% coverage for dependents
    • Company HSA contribution with the High Deductible Health Plan

  • 401(k) Retirement Plan (employer will match contribution up to 3.5% of employee contribution)

  • Basic Life, Voluntary Life and AD&D Insurance options

  • Supplemental, voluntary benefits

    • Student Loan Repayment/529 Education Savings with a monthly company contribution
    • FSA (Medical, Dependent, Transit and Parking)
    • Voluntary Life and AD&D Insurance
    • Critical Illness Insurance
    • Accident Insurance
    • Short- and Long-term Disability Insurance
    • Pet Insurance
    • Identity theft protection
    • Legal access to a network of attorneys

  • PTO, paid sick leave, and company holidays (including a 2026 holiday shutdown)

  • Paid parental leave

Why Work at Weedmaps?

Life at Weedmaps means innovation and heart. Come join us if you care about the plant, the people who love it, and are ready to let your talent shine. We foster a bustling and collaborative culture that revolves around an environment that focuses on the benefits of weed, and the community that supports it.

You too can have a hand in shaping the industry’s future; ready to roll with us?

See how we’ve grown—our journey, leadership team, and what’s next atWeedmaps.com/corporate

About Weedmaps:

Founded in 2008, we’ve grown from a small startup to a global leader in the cannabis industry.  Our dedication to transparency, education, and community has set us apart, and today, we proudly serve cannabis to consumers and businesses in the U.S. and worldwide.

“Freedom to choose. Freedom to access. Freedom to enjoy.”

Notice to prospective Weedmaps job applicants:

Our team has been made aware of incidents involving LinkedIn, Telegram, and Facebook accounts impersonating Weedmaps recruiters. These individuals are attempting to use our company name to solicit payment from prospective candidates interested in applying for jobs at our company. Our team is actively working to combat these attempts, but in the meantime, please be mindful of the following:

  • Our recruiters will always communicate with candidates through an @weedmaps.com email address.

  • CORRECT: jlebowski@weedmaps.com

  • INCORRECT: jlebowski@gmail.com

  • Our recruiters will NEVER ask for or attempt to solicit payment from applicants in order to apply, interview, or work for Weedmaps.

  • If you are interested in a role at Weedmaps, please apply through our established channels.

  • Weedmaps Careers Page or LinkedIn

If you are unsure if a communication is legitimate, please contact our recruitment team at talentops@weedmaps.com and they will happily confirm for you. Thank you for your vigilance and we appreciate your interest in working with us!

Weedmaps is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. The Company prohibits unlawful discrimination against employees or applicants based on race (including traits historically associated with race, such as hair texture and protective hairstyles), religion and religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, military status, veteran status, uniformed service member status, sexual orientation, transgender identity, citizenship status, pregnancy, or any other consideration made unlawful by federal, state, or local laws. The Company also prohibits unlawful discrimination based on the perception that anyone has any of those characteristics, or is associated with a person who has or is perceived as having any of those characteristics. Our company uses E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. To learn more about E-Verify, including your rights and responsibilities, please visit www.dhs.gov/E-Verify.

Applicants are entitled to reasonable accommodations under the terms of the Americans with Disabilities Act and applicable state/local laws, unless the accommodation presents undue hardship. Please email us at peopleoperations at weedmaps.com if you would like to confidentially discuss a potential accommodation during the interview process.

jobs.title: Technical Project Manager
jobs.jd_full:
WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands.

We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth.

We have been building the world’s most valuable brands for 50 years and have global reach across 100+ markets, with deep local expertise.

Our people are the key to our success. We’re committed to fostering a culture of creativity, belonging and continuous learning, attracting and developing the brightest talent, and providing exciting career opportunities that help our people grow.

For more information, visit WPP.com.

Why we’re hiring:

As part of our People Analytics team, you’ll be working closely with the People team, Data & Tech teams as well as other broader functions across our group and operating companies to help support our reporting and data analytics. We are undergoing a large transformation across our People and Finance functions and the People Analytics team has a vast array of projects with touchpoints across multiple functions. We need a project manager to get stuck in and help us deliver projects that have never been done before.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Liaise with c-suite executives to co-ordinate the creation, build, production and continuous improvement of analytics products
  •  Manage and scope out projects that span across functions • Drive project success metrics and timelines
  • Identify opportunities to improve operational efficiencies, improve quality, and implement scalable solutions across our agencies
  • Identify and proactively handle risks, issues, dependencies, forecast resource demand and prioritisation
  • Directly manage the day to day of our analytics team

What you’ll need:

Minimum requirements for this role include:

  • 3+ years experience in a project management role and utilising agile methodologies
  • Experience working with a cross-functional team
  • Excellent communication skills and an ability to collaborate and engage with the C-suite
  • Experience with data-driven decision making and problem solving
  • Both detail and ‘big picture’ conscious
  • The competence, confidence, and self-discipline to manage deadlines, estimates, and budgets excellently
  • Diligent and attentive to detail, able to listen, learn, and put into practice
  • Proficiency in Excel
  • Stakeholder Engagement & Relationships
  • Full Product Lifecycle Management
  • Microsoft Tools, MS Project, MoSCoW
  • Team Leadership & Team Management
  • IT, Technology & Business Alignment
  • Change Management
  • CSCS, H&S, IIF, Scrum, Agile, PRINCE2, ITIL
  • Quality Management, ISO 9000
  • Risk Management, Risk Mitigation, Compliance
  • Re-Baseline & Review Processes
  • Business Cases & Benefits Realisation
  • Business Process Re-engineering
  • Curiosity to learn!

Additional preferred skills:

  • Experience working with data teams (engineers, analysts, reporters, data scientists, etc.)
  • Experience operating on agile cross-functional teams
  • Presentation skills with a high degree of comfort with both large and small audiences

Who you are:

You’re open : We are inclusive and collaborative; we encourage the free exchange of ideas; we respect and celebrate diverse views. We are open-minded: to new ideas, new partnerships, new ways of working.

You’re optimistic : We believe in the power of creativity, technology and talent to create brighter futures or our people, our clients and our communities. We approach all that we do with conviction: to try the new and to seek the unexpected.

You’re extraordinary: we are stronger together: through collaboration we achieve the amazing. We are creative leaders and pioneers of our industry; we provide extraordinary every day.

What we’ll give you:

Passionate, inspired people – We aim to create a culture in which people can do extraordinary work.

Scale and opportunity – We offer the opportunity to create, influence and complete projects at a scale that is unparalleled in the industry.

Challenging and stimulating work – Unique work and the opportunity to join a group of creative problem solvers. Are you up for the challenge?

#LI-Hybrid

We believe the best work happens when we’re together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That’s why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

WPP is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

Please read our Privacy Notice (https://www.wpp.com/en/careers/wpp-privacy-policy-for-recruitment) for more information on how we process the information you provide.

jobs.title: Technical Product Owner
jobs.jd_full:
Founded in 2017, TripArc is a $750 million privately held company powers the top Travel Agencies and Tour Operators in North America. Our expansive and growing network empowers seamless access to a comprehensive range of travel options and services globally with over $750 million transactions annually - all at the industry’s best rates.

As an industry leader in technology, purchasing, finance, HR and marketing, we know how to deliver solutions to travel professionals in the most effective way possible. With deep experience in partnering with two leading brands in the luxury travel space – Kensington Tours and Travel Edge – TripArc has real insight into the needs of tour operators and travel agencies today.

TripArc’s goals are simple. To make our network of over 1400 travel entities more profitable, efficient and help them create truly unique travel for their clients.

TripArc connects travel advisors to a broad network of supplier content, including tours, activities, and experiences sourced through Destination Management Companies (DMCs). DMCs are specialist operators that curate and deliver local experiences in a given destination — think guided tours, transfers, excursions, and curated itineraries. TripArc aggregates content from multiple DMC suppliers through a centralized integration layer, normalizing supplier data into a consistent format that advisors can search, quote, and book through the platform. The Technical Product Owner in this role will own the product backlog for this aggregation work, managing how new DMC supplier APIs are evaluated, onboarded, and mapped into TripArc’s internal data model.

The Technical Product Owner is solely responsible for maintaining the Product Backlog at a pod level, using input from the Senior Product Manager, the development team, the Solutions & Support team, procurement, and supplier partners to prioritize and communicate backlog items ahead of all Scrum ceremonies. They ensure the Product Backlog is visible, transparent, and understood at all times. Even where responsibilities such as technical ticket writing are delegated to a developer lead or architect, the Product Owner remains accountable for the outcome.

Working within the Trifecta (Product, UX, Development) model, the Technical Product Owner anticipates the needs of stakeholders across the organization, including those not directly involved in day-to-day delivery, and ensures that all requests for backlog changes are evaluated and approved through them.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct needs assessments with internal stakeholders — including the Senior Product Manager, Solutions & Support, and Procurement to identify business requirements and translate them into features or user stories
  • Evaluate new supplier and aggregator API documentation independently; identify field mapping gaps against TripArc’s internal data model and produce structured integration specifications for the development team
  • Collaborate with the architect and technical lead on API contract decisions, authentication patterns, rate limiting, and resilience considerations
  • Determine scope and prepare requirements documents for integration projects to ensure business and technical requirements are understood and accepted by stakeholders
  • Create and manage the Product Backlog, including prioritization, grooming, and communication to the pod ahead of all Scrum ceremonies
  • Use tools such as Postman to test and validate API endpoints and responses prior to handing off to the development team
  • Review and contribute to OpenAPI/Swagger specifications in support of integration design decisions
  • Write acceptance criteria and user stories with sufficient technical detail to eliminate ambiguity for the development team
  • Prepare detailed flow charts and diagrams outlining system capabilities and integration processes
  • Liaise with external supplier and aggregator partners on API availability, onboarding requirements, and technical queries
  • Coordinate with the Solutions & Support team and other internal stakeholders to ensure integration changes are understood and supportable
  • Investigate and analyze problems, business processes, and requirements in order to recommend appropriate solutions
  • Document system problems, integration decisions, and resolutions for future reference
  • Support the Senior Product Manager in the product roadmap process and long-term integration planning
  • Prepare and facilitate planning and refinement ceremonies within the Scrum team

Key Skills / Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Science, Computer Science, or a related field; a

combination of education and suitable work experience will be considered

  • 3–5 years of experience in a Product Owner, Technical Business Analyst, or integration focused product role
  • Demonstrated experience reading and interpreting REST API documentation and mapping responses against an internal data model
  • Hands-on experience using Postman or equivalent tooling for API testing and validation
  • Working knowledge of OpenAPI/Swagger specifications, including the ability to review and contribute to API contract documents
  • Experience producing structured integration specifications or technical requirements documents that engineering teams can act on directly
  • Familiarity with aggregator or multi-supplier integration patterns, including supplier normalization, availability caching, and booking and cancellation flows
  • Strong backlog management and prioritization skills with experience facilitating Scrum ceremonies
  • Ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Skilled in structured business analysis on medium to large integration projects
  • Excellent organization, planning, and written and verbal communication skills
  • Experience in the travel industry is an asset; equivalent experience in e-commerce, logistics, fintech, or marketplace platform integration will be considered

Cultural Fit

  • Thrives working in a technology and KPI-driven organization
  • Able to thrive in an entrepreneurial environment
  • Highly functional in a fast-paced, constantly changing workplace — building plans through iterations based on what is and is not working
  • Ability to build trust and work through conflict both upwards and downward

We know that our success is dependent on the people who join our team, which is why we recruit the best. Our team is made up of owners — people who are smart, low ego, and accountable for their results. We all play a part in the success of the company and are proud of what we do!

We provide a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. Employees have the opportunity to participate in incentive programs and compensation tied to business and individual performance. The expected compensation base for this position is starting at $100,000 CAD.

The actual compensation may vary depending on local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualifications, experience, and education/training.

#LI-Hybrid

We are committed to providing employment accommodation in accordance with the Ontario Human Rights Code and the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act. If you require accommodation due to a disability at any stage of our hiring process, please advise us when completing your application.

The Range Group may use artificial intelligence throughout the recruitment process to screen, assess or select applicants for this position. These tools assist our hiring team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. We thank all candidates for their interest however only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager Source Control and Actions
jobs.jd_full:
About us

Copia Automation provides best-in-class disaster and recovery technology for industrial automation.

Our product brings modern developer tools to the OT space through our novel Git-based source control for automation professionals, with multiple delivery methods including self-hosted and fully hosted options. We are a well-funded startup with a growing list of happy customers throughout the industrial space.

As a Copia employee, you’ll solve challenging, unique, and meaningful problems alongside passionate coworkers, while we change the way everything gets built.

About the Role

We’re seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead product strategy and execution for Copia’s Source Control product and our newest product, Copia Actions. Source Control is Copia’s flagship product, used by automation engineers to branch, diff, review, and version their PLC and automation code. Copia Actions is a brand-new product that extends Source Control with automated workflows: a CI/CD layer for industrial automation, where customers can trigger

  • Working with the Head of Product, you’ll develop actionable hypotheses that guide our product initiatives, contributing to product roadmaps and strategy.
  • In partnership with engineering, you’ll prioritize and manage the project backlog in alignment with the product roadmap.

You’ll work closely with engineering to define requirements and create specifications for new projects or features.

  • You will manage customer feedback channels and market research activities, using data to validate or adjust product iterations.
  • You will work cross-functionally with marketing, success, and engineering for product launch planning.
  • Own the Source Control roadmap: Define and prioritize improvements across branching, diffing, code review, version history, and integrations with the PLC IDEs and automation platforms our customers use every day.
  • Lead Copia Actions from 0→1: Define the MVP, identify the right use cases (e.g., automated testing of PLC code, deployment pipelines, governance and compliance checks), and bring the product through alpha, beta, and GA.
  • Be the voice of the automation engineer: Understand how controls engineers, PLC programmers, and OT teams ship code today, and where the workflow breaks down.
  • Drive cross-functional impact: Partner with other product teams (Platform, DeviceLink, Copilot) so Source Control and Actions fit cleanly into the rest of Copia.
  • Customer & stakeholder engagement: Act as both internal product expert and external face of the product, working directly with customers, solution engineers, partners, and leadership.
  • Metrics & outcomes: Establish KPIs for Source Control engagement (commits, PRs, reviews) and Copia Actions adoption (workflows created, runs); measure impact on retention and customer satisfaction.

About You

  • You have 5-7 years of hands-on product management experience, with at least 2 years focused on developer tools, source control, CI/CD, or developer productivity products. Ideally, you have experience in fast-paced product organizations or high-growth startups.
    • You thrive in a startup environment and are resourceful, adaptable, and execution-driven.
  • You have taken at least one product or major feature from 0→1 and seen it through to GA.
  • You create clarity out of ambiguity.
  • You’re an excellent communicator, skilled at aligning various stakeholders around immediate objectives and deliverables.
  • Your low ego makes you receptive to feedback and agile in adjusting plans.
  • You have strong technical acumen—you can engage deeply with engineering on Git internals, CI/CD systems, and developer tooling tradeoffs.
  • You’ve worked closely with engineers as your end users.
  • You’ve proven your ability to translate technical capabilities into customer and business value.

Bonus Points

  • Background in industrial automation, manufacturing systems, or OT/IT convergence.
  • Experience as a PM (or close partner to PMs) on a developer tool such as GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Atlassian, Linear, or similar.
  • Experience with CI/CD or workflow automation products (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Jenkins, GitLab CI, Argo, etc.).
  • Familiarity with Git workflows, code review practices, and DevOps culture.

$160,000 - $180,000 a year

Benefits include unlimited PTO, employer-subsidized healthcare through Aetna, commuter benefits, in-office lunches, and more! Full-time, permanent employees also receive generous equity packages.

**This position is hybrid out of our New York City HQ.**

Come join us!

Copia is growing extraordinarily fast! Join a best-in-class start-up with huge amounts of upside and impact. Our headquarters is in New York City.

jobs.title: Junior Project Manager Intern
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Entra a far parte del nostro team! In Auto 1 Group avrai l’opportunità di lavorare a stretto contatto con professionisti appassionati, utilizzando strumenti e metodi all’avanguardia per fare davvero la differenza. Grazie al tuo contributo, supporterai in modo trasversale sia il dipartimento Sales che il dipartimento Remarketing, collaborando su progetti strategici di analisi e sviluppo del business. Potrai esprimere tutto il tuo talento nella gestione di dati, processi e risorse, costruendo insieme a noi un percorso di crescita solido e stimolante.

Cosa Offriamo

  • Contratto di Internship;
  • Lavoro ibrido con SmartWorking 2 giorni  a settimana: organizza al meglio il tuo tempo e il tuo equilibrio vita-lavoro tra la comodità del remote e la vitalità del nostro HQ a Milano.
  • Team giovane e ambiente smart: lavorerai in un contesto fresco, dinamico, informale e attento alle persone;
  • Avrai accesso a reali opportunità di sviluppo, in un ambiente internazionale e meritocratico dove ogni contributo fa la differenza.

Job Description

  • Gestirai progetti innovativi dall’idea al lancio: incontrerai stakeholder interni, definirai i requisiti con i developer, monitorerai test e release.
  • Redigerai documenti chiave come forecast, report e SAL.
  • Analizzerai insieme al management l’efficacia commerciale e i KPI.
  • Supporterai il team sales con la tua capacità nell’analisi ed elaborazione di dati.
  • Userai strumenti avanzati di project management e la suite Google per tenere tutto sotto controllo..
  • Creerai report di mercato, presentazioni efficaci e comunicazioni ad hoc per la direzione

Qualifications

  • Laurea triennale in discipline economiche o ingegneristiche
  • Esperienza in aziende multinazionali o società di consulenza
  • Ottima conoscenza di inglese e Pacchetto Office (Excel/PowerPoint top priority); conoscenza di Suite Google e SQL è un plus
  • Proattività, problem solving e voglia di fare la differenza!

Additional Information

Vuoi fare la differenza e crescere con noi?

Unisciti al nostro team e aiutaci a ridisegnare il futuro della dell’automotive in Italia.

Luogo di lavoro: Via Perin Del Vaga 8, Milano - Zona Certosa.

Orario di lavoro: Full time 40h a settimana, flessibilità oraria in ingresso tra le 8.00-10.00.

Il presente annuncio è rivolto ad entrambi i sessi, ai sensi delle leggi 903⁄77 e 125⁄91, e a persone di tutte le età e tutte le nazionalità, ai sensi dei decreti legislativi 215⁄03 e 216⁄03.
